title: requests — Laravel notifications
description: Five notifications this module owns. All classified `transactional` (essential) — access requests are directly-solicited communications tied to the platform's authorization loop, so opt-out does not apply to the request itself. Long-form marketing / product communications from Stackra require explicit consent captured on user provisioning, which pre-dates every access request.

notifications.description: Sent to each approver when a request is submitted. Body: requester name + email, resource type + display_name, permissions_wanted (rendered as human-readable labels via `access/rbac::PermissionLabels`), the requester's reason (verbatim), CTA button linking to `/tenant/access-requests/{id}` for the approve/reject decision. The mail body includes a 'you can approve directly from this email' fallback link (deep-link that opens the SPA approver decision panel). Localised to the approver's preferred locale.

Sent to the requester when their request is approved. Body: 'Your access to X was approved by Y'. Includes a CTA button linking BACK to the original resource URL that emitted the 403 that started the whole flow — the requester's typical action is to retry the resource fetch. Also mentions the grant's TTL ('This access expires on <date>').

Sent when the request is rejected. Body includes the rejecting approver's comment verbatim (SOC 2 requires a rationale on every reject, so the comment is always present). Suggests next steps: 'if you still need this access, please reach out to your admin directly'. No CTA button — the flow is terminal.

Sent when a pending request expires without decision. Body: 'Your request for X received no decision within N hours and has expired.' CTA button: 'Submit a new request' (pre-fills the form with the same payload as the original). Also suggests reaching out to the admin directly — an expired request often signals an unresponsive approver group and the requester needs a human intervention.

targets: User (each pending approver of an old request)
notifications.description: Nightly reminder to approvers who haven't decided on a pending request older than `config('access.requests.notifications.reminder_after_hours')` (default 24h). Body: 'X is still waiting on your decision — they submitted this <N hours> ago and it expires in <M hours>.' Bumps to a stronger 'this is your last reminder' variant on the third + final sending. Max reminders: `config('access.requests.notifications.max_reminders')` (default 3). Gated by the `access_request_reminders` entitlement (medium+ tiers only).

consentTierNotes.transactional: Directly-solicited communications essential to the authorization loop. Not gated by marketing opt-out. Access-request notifications are transactional at every touchpoint — approvers receive them because they hold administrative responsibility; requesters receive them because they initiated the flow.
